name: Chennai Petroleum Corporation Ltd
oneLine: WordPress fingerprint exposed via /wp-json/; missing CSP

tls.issuer: Sectigo Public Server Auth CA OV R36

findings: WordPress fingerprint exposed via Link headers (rel=alternate to /wp-json/v2)
Permissions-Policy permits payment and fullscreen — broader than typical

versions: WordPress version detectable via Link headers (wp-json)
Sectigo Public Server Auth CA OV R36 TLS (expires 2026-10-25)

attackPaths.name: WordPress version detection → CVE matching
attackPaths.entry: Identify WP version via wp-json. Enumerate plugins (King Addons, WPBakery).
attackPaths.pivot: Match to CVE-2025-8489 (King Addons CVSS 9.8 KEV — privilege escalation) or CVE-2024-56286 (WPBakery CVSS 8.2 stored XSS).

attackPaths.name: King Addons CVE-2025-8489 unauthenticated privilege escalation
attackPaths.entry: Free user registration (if enabled). Specify 'administrator' role in payload.

attackPaths.name: WPBakery CVE-2024-56286 stored XSS
attackPaths.entry: Legacy plugin contains stored XSS in page builder.
attackPaths.pivot: Inject payload via contributor/editor account; admin views; token harvested.

attackPaths.name: Soft-fail SPF + Office 365 MX → spear phishing
attackPaths.entry: SPF ~all soft-fail; Office 365 MX shared infrastructure.
attackPaths.pivot: Spoofed mail from cpcl-alternate.mail.protection.outlook.com may pass alignment.
attackPaths.objective: Reach inbox with valid SPF; supplier-payment fraud.

paths.preAi: WordPress version detection → plugin CVE matching → privilege escalation
paths.mythos: AI-augmented attacker auto-correlates wp-json fingerprint with current CVE chain in seconds

paths.preAi: Soft-fail SPF + O365 MX → spear-phishing with high deliverability

cisoPatchList.title: Audit WordPress core + all plugins; update against CVE-2025-8489
cisoPatchList.fix: wp-cli: wp plugin list --update=available. Update King Addons → 51.1.35+. Update WPBakery / Elementor / others. Test on staging.
cisoPatchList.validation: wp plugin status: all current; vulnerable plugin versions absent

cisoPatchList.title: Suppress WordPress version disclosure
cisoPatchList.fix: Remove Link headers (wp-json). Disable /wp-json/ or require auth. Hide version in HTML meta + HTTP headers.

cisoPatchList.title: Disable or restrict user registration
cisoPatchList.fix: WP Admin → Settings → Membership uncheck 'Anyone can register'. If needed, allowlist @cpcl.co.in only with manual approval.

cisoPatchList.title: Add CSP header — nonce-based
cisoPatchList.validation: curl -i shows CSP with nonce, no unsafe-inline/eval in script-src

cisoPatchList.title: Upgrade SPF from ~all to -all after 30-day monitoring
cisoPatchList.fix: Monitor SPF alignment 30 days; replace ~all with -all.

cisoPatchList.title: Upgrade DMARC p=quarantine → p=reject
cisoPatchList.fix: After SPF -all + 30-day clean rua, promote DMARC to p=reject.

cisoPatchList.title: Deploy WAF — block WordPress exploitation patterns
cisoPatchList.fix: Cloudflare or ModSecurity OWASP CRS. Custom rules: block /wp-admin/admin-ajax.php admin payloads from non-admin sessions.

cisoPatchList.title: Audit WordPress user roles + enforce 2FA on admins
cisoPatchList.fix: WP Admin → Users → audit each role + last login + 2FA status. Remove inactive users. Wordfence or Two-Factor plugin.

cisoPatchList.title: DKIM signing for Office 365 mail
cisoPatchList.fix: O365 admin: Enable DKIM. Verify alignment with DMARC.

phase2.headlineQuestion: Can CPCL patch King Addons (CVE-2025-8489 CVSS 9.8 KEV) and suppress WordPress version disclosure before the next reconnaissance scan triggers automated exploitation?
